item.title: Breaking Kinetic Symmetry: How Wet–Dry Cycling Ratchets Prebiotic Chemistry Far from Equilibrium
item.description: Wet-dry cycling drives formation of biological oligomers from unactivated monomers, but the mechanism of directional accumulation has remained unclear. Low water activity and high monomer concentrations favor condensation; high water activity and low ...

item.title: Assembly of Membranized Coacervates through Transmembrane Peptides
Mucus Anchoring Protein (MAP) – Antibody Conjugates: A Strategy to Enhance Antiviral Efficacy
item.description: Respiratory viruses initiate infections at the airway mucosa, where improved localization and retention of neutralizing antibodies at the infection interface may enhance antiviral activity under mucus-containing conditions. To enhance antibody ...

item.title: An element-resolved coupled cluster atomization energy data set ranging across the periodic table
item.description: We introduce the total atomization energy periodic table compass (TAE-PTComp), a collection of 67 atomization energy data sets, each specifically designed to probe various chemical environments of a characteristic element of the periodic table. In total, ...

item.title: High-resolution MALDI imaging mass spectrometry of wholebody adult zebrafish
item.description: We developed an optimized sample preparation workflow tailored for high-spatial resolution matrix-assisted laser desorption/ionization imaging mass spectrometry (MALDI IMS) of adult zebrafish tissue. Zebrafish (Danio rerio) are an important model organism ...

item.title: Proteomics as an Emerging Tool for Understanding Biotransformation and Toxicological Impacts of Contaminants
item.description: Mass-spectrometry-based proteomics is rapidly advancing, becoming fast, affordable, and increasingly accessible to environmental scientists. This perspective outlines how proteomics can illuminate biotransformation processes and sublethal organism-level ...

item.title: Structure-based virtual screening identifies structurally novel, parasite-selective inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase
item.description: Background. Dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) is a clinically validated antimalarial target: Plasmodium falciparum depends on de novo pyrimidine biosynthesis and, unlike the human host, cannot salvage pyrimidines. The leading inhibitor chemotype — the ...

item.title: Systematic Expansion of Chemical Reaction Space from Graph-Based Model Reactions
item.description: Computational kinetic modeling relies on accurate transition-state geometries and activation barriers. While machine learning models trained on ab initio data have shown promise for transition-state search and barrier prediction, they often struggle to ...

item.title: OPTIMIZATION OF WASTE ENGINE OIL REGENERATION USING ACID-ACTIVATED BENTONITE
item.description: The conditions for deep acid activation of natural bentonite have been substantiated, and its effectiveness in the process of regenerating waste engine oil has been investigated. It has been established that the modification leads to a radical ...

item.title: Straightforward structure determination of beam-sensitive low-symmetry compounds at room temperature by precession SerialED
item.description: SerialED is acknowledged as an easily automatable alternative to 3DED, especially promising for the structure elucidation of very beam sensitive samples, like pharmaceuticals, porous framework materials for catalysis and macromolecules. Yet, SerialED in ...

item.title: Practical One-pot Synthesis of Cyclic Conjugated Amides from Ketones
item.description: Conjugated amides are valuable building blocks in organic synthesis; however, general access to cyclic conjugated amides remains limited, restricting their broader use in method development and complex molecule synthesis. Herein, we report a practical one-...

item.title: Biophysical Descriptors of Single Protein Domains
item.description: Quantitative relationships among geometric and surface-chemical descriptors of single-domain proteins remain insufficiently characterized, despite their relevance for interpreting structural datasets and modeling biomolecular interactions. Using a curated,...

item.title: Deciphering the anion dynamics in the fully deuterated pyridinium-based ionic liquid [C5Py][NTf2] via 19F fast field cycling NMR relaxometry and MD simulation
item.description: Fast Field Cycling (FFC) NMR relaxometry allows the investigation of molecular dynamics over a broad frequency range from 10 kHz up to 100 MHz. However, quantitative data interpretation is often limited by system-specific motional models and simplified ...

item.title: Genetically-targeted and red-emitting fluorescent indicators for subcellular zinc imaging
item.description: The concentration of biologically relevant metal cations can be monitored with subcellular precision thanks to chemogenetic fluorescent indicators that combine a small molecular probe with a recombinant protein tag but, despite a few reported examples ...

item.title: Why a Single Mutation Dims the Light: Local Chromophore Organization Modulates Fluorescence in smURFP
item.description: Predicting mutation-induced photophysical changes in bilin-binding fluorescent proteins remains challenging. Here, we investigate the origin of fluorescence modulation in smURFP and its lowfluorescent-quantum-yield Y56R variant using classical MD ...

item.title: Data-Efficient and Fast Machine Learning Molecular Dynamics through Integrated Active Learning and Knowledge Distillation
item.description: We develop data-efficient machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) for fast molecular dynamics simulations, combining DeePMD and MACE models within an active learning and knowledge distillation framework. Using liquid water as a case study, we ...

item.title: Distribution and Function of Pyrimidine Nucleoside 2'-Hydroxylases across the Tree of Life
item.description: Pyrimidine nucleoside metabolism is classically divided into phosphorolytic and hydrolytic pathways mediated by nucleoside phosphorylases and nucleoside hydrolases (NSHs), respectively. While ribonucleoside turnover is well characterized, it remains ...

item.title: Correlating the distribution of site energies, activation energies and saddle point energies in the energy landscape of a lithium borate glass
item.description: Long range transport of ions in a solid electrolyte is subject to the potential energy landscape in which local hopping of ions occurs. Local hopping occurs between neighboring sites (energy minima). It involves passing a local saddle point implying the ...

item.title: Designer Pseudopeptide for In Situ Liquid Crystal Gelation toward Smart Window Applications
item.description: Supramolecular self-assembly serves as a fundamental backbone for designing multifunctional gel materials. A simpler diphenylalanine (Phe-Phe) sequence has been strategically modified with distinct terminal functional groups, namely, tert-butoxycarbonyl (...

item.description: Decades of origin-of-life research have uncovered hundreds of prebiotically plausible reactions across hydrothermal, surface, and biochemical environments, yet this knowledge remains fragmented across chemistry, geochemistry, and astrobiology. Here we ...

item.description: The controlled organization of chromophores into defined assemblies with collective photophysical properties is a key concept for natural and artificial photonic systems. A template-free, one-step synthesis affords a series of methylene-bridged ...

item.description: The Critical Assessments of Computational Hit-finding Experiments (CACHE) are prospective benchmarking exercises where small molecule ligands predicted for a nominated protein of interest are procured and tested experimentally. In CACHE #4, 23 ...

item.description: Efficient triplet-state harvesting in organic chromophores is governed by a delicate interplay between excited-state energetics, orbital character, spin–orbit coupling, and vibronic relaxation pathways. Here, we present a systematic theoretical ...

item.description: A regioselective 1,3-aminoarylation of aryl cyclopropanes is reported through two complementary catalytic strategies based on distinct reactivity. First, photoactivation of N-chloro-N-(phenylsulfonyl)benzenesulfonamide (NCBSI) enables a rapid and ...

item.description: Photopharmacology enables precise optical control of biological function through lightresponsive ligands, yet multiplexed photocontrol has been constrained by limited wavelength selectivity and reliance on UV-activated photocages. Here we report a three-...

item.title: A Nickel-Olefin Pincer Complex as Catalyst for Aldehyde Hydrogenation under Mild Conditions via a Cooperative Mechanism
item.description: The use of a Ni(0)-olefin pincer complex for the base-free catalytic hydrogenation of aldehydes under mild conditions is reported. Insights from DFT calculations complemented by isotope labeling experiments support an unusual H2 activation mechanism ...

item.title: Arginine tuning of coiled-coil peptides yields single-domain nanocrystals
item.description: Programming three-dimensional order in peptide-based materials requires lateral interactions that are precise enough to propagate over hundreds of nanometres, yet robust enough to operate in water. Here, we show that a rationally designed coiled-coil ...

item.title: BEAD: a Human-in-the-Loop Agentic Framework for Explainable and Prospective Drug Discovery
item.description: A critical bottleneck in AI-driven drug discovery is the inability to integrate traditional medicinal chemistry intuition, which frequently results in uninterpretable or chemically implausible molecular outputs. Here, we present BEAD (Bridging the gap ...

item.title: Polyanion-Based Compounds for Rechargeable Magnesium Batteries
item.description: Rechargeable magnesium metal batteries represent a compelling post-lithium energy storage technology owing to the abundance of terrestrial magnesium reserves, their current cost-effectiveness and intrinsically higher safety relative to lithium systems, ...

item.title: Asymmetric Alkene Isomerization Approach to C4-Substituted Piperidines
item.description: Chiral C4-substituted piperidines are prominent motifs in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and alkaloid natural products, yet broadly applicable strategies for their asymmetric synthesis and diversification remain underdeveloped. Here we report a catalytic ...

item.title: Intrinsic Irreducible-Cartesian Tensor Carrier for Equivariant Interatomic Potentials
item.description: Equivariant interatomic potentials need compact angular features, while many physical observables and long-range sources are Cartesian tensors. We introduce the intrinsic irreducible-Cartesian tensor carrier (ICTC), which builds harmonic polynomial bases ...

item.title: Nanoscale Strain-Engineering Controls Hydrogen Reactivity in Nanoparticle Catalysts
item.description: Nanoparticle catalysts empower clean and energy-efficient chemical production, with their performance traditionally tuned through particle size, composition, and support interactions. Lattice strain has emerged as an additional handle to control catalytic ...

item.title: Optimizing the Thermoelectric Performance of the Earth-Abundant Layered Phosphide BaCuP through Defect Engineering
item.description: Thermoelectric materials offer a direct route for converting waste heat into electricity, but the discovery of efficient, sustainable, and non-toxic compounds remains a major challenge. Here, we investigate the thermoelectric potential of the layered 1-1-...

item.title: Zinc-Air Micro-batteries in Tear Fluid for Contact Lens Electronics
item.description: Contact lenses are emerging as a promising platform for wearable electronics, but the design and scaling of power sources remain important considerations due to constraints in size, biocompatibility, and limits on thermal dissipation. Herein, we ...

item.title: Thermodynamic Path Memory in Coarse-Grained Stochastic Molecular Systems
item.description: Experiments on small nonequilibrium, chemical, and biophysical systems often record a coarse observable rather than the full mesoscopic state, making it unclear whether the measured present is sufficient to predict future response. We study this ...

item.title: Is One Molecular Representation Enough? A Comparative Study of Featurization Methods Across Diverse Datasets
item.description: The predictive performance of molecular machine learning models depends on how molecules are featurized, yet fair comparisons across methods are rare because studies often vary architectures and hyperparameters. We benchmark five molecular featurization ...

item.title: Molecular Thermodynamics of Lower Critical Solution Temperature Ionic Liquid/Water Mixtures: Tetrabutylphosphonium Trifluoroacetate
item.description: Lower critical solution temperature (LCST) ionic liquids (ILs) are thermoresponsive materials that undergo liquid–liquid demixing with water into water-rich (WR) and IL-rich (ILR) phases upon heating. Despite the vast combinatorial space of cation–anion ...

item.title: Crystal Structure of a Ytterbium(III)–Pyrroloquinoline Quinone (PQQ) Complex
item.description: Pyrroloquinoline quinone (PQQ) is a redox-active quinone-based cofactor best known for its role in bacterial alcohol dehydrogenases, including a subset of lanthanide-dependent enzymes discovered in recent years. To gain further structural insight into ...

item.title: Machine Learning Prediction of Ionic Conductivity in Solid-State Electrolytes Using Compositional, Structural, and Neural Potential Features
item.description: Identifying solid-state electrolyte (SSE) materials with high ionic conductivity is critical for next-generation lithium-ion batteries, yet experimental discovery remains slow and resource-intensive. Here we present a machine learning pipeline that ...

item.title: Functionalization and Mechanical Properties of Poly(lactide-cyclohexadiene oxide) Block Copolymers
item.description: A series of lactide (LA) and cyclohexadiene oxide (CHDO) multiblock copolymers was synthesized using a redox switchable complex, (salfen)Zr(OiPr)2 (salfen = N,N’-bis(2,4-di-tert-butylphenoxy)-1,1’-ferrocenediimine), with LA being polymerized by the ...

item.title: A modular skill and orchestration framework for traceable agentic computational chemistry
item.description: Large-language-model agents can now operate the software of computational chemistry, but how their science compares with the answers human experts produced remains untested. Here we present AI Computational Chemist (AICC), a modular skill framework that ...

item.title: Understanding and Reporting PFAS Exposure: Wide-scope Retrospective Suspect Screening of High-resolution Mass Spectrometry Data from Exposomics Studies
item.description: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) comprise thousands of persistent and structurally diverse chemicals that contribute to chronic human exposure. Yet, routine biomonitoring targets only a limited set of regulated legacy compounds, underestimating ...

item.title: Antibacterial Activity of Metal-Free Bipyridines: Vinylpyridine versus Methyl Substituents
item.description: In this work, we report that incorporating a vinylpyridine arm into 4,4′-dimethyl-2,2′-bipyridine (L1) significantly enhances antibacterial potency against both Gram-positive (B. subtilis,S. epidermidis,S. aureus) and Gram-negative (S. enterica,E. coli)...

item.title: β-Stereoselective Glycosylation without Acyl Participation: Evaluation of β-(1→2)-Linked Disaccharide Donors and Their Application to Glycoarray Design for Antibody Characterization
item.description: β-(1→2)-Glucans constitute a relatively rare and substantially underexplored class of polysaccharides. Of particular interest is the macrocyclic β-(1→2)-glucan from Brucella (CβG), which regarded as a promising diagnostic marker of brucellosis. However, ...

item.title: Molecular Motion-Activated Spin-Vibronic Coupling: A Pre-Twisting Design Strategy for Efficient Pure Organic Room Temperature Phosphorescence
item.description: Suppressing molecular motion in the solid state has long been regarded as a golden key for achieving efficient room temperature phosphorescence (RTP), as it minimizes the rates of nonradiative decay and external quenching. Here we demonstrate a ...

item.title: Inverse theoretical spectroscopy via Monte Carlo sampling: From spectra to ensembles of molecular structures
item.description: A molecule’s structure determines its spectroscopic properties, and vice versa, molecular spectra are indispensable for elucidating its three-dimensional structure. Here, we present a Monte Carlo sampling scheme for generating an ensemble of molecular ...

item.title: Atomically precise metal nanoclusters in nitrate-to-ammonia electrocatalysis
item.description: Driven by the growing need for sustainable ammonia production and effective nitrate remediation, the electrocatalytic nitrate reduction reaction (NO3RR) has emerged as a promising technology. This process enables the conversion of nitrate pollutants into ...

item.title: Proteomics as an Emerging Tool for Understanding Biotransformation and Toxicological Impacts of Contaminants
item.description: Mass-spectrometry-based proteomics is rapidly advancing, becoming fast, affordable, and increasingly accessible to environmental scientists. This perspective outlines how proteomics can illuminate biotransformation processes and sublethal organism-level ...

item.title: Switching Reactivity with Light: C–P Bonding Controls Singlet Oxygen Generation and Reactivity in DHP–CPD Photoswitches
item.description: The dimethyldihydropyrene photochromic system is a 14π-electronic framework that switches to a lessaromatic non-planar system with visible light. In this work we unravel a neutral phosphorus heteroatom-appended dimethyldihydropyrene (DHP) photoswitchable ...

item.title: Metagenomics and Machine Learning for Per-and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS): A Review of Convergent Approaches, 2020–2025
item.description: Per-and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are persistent, mobile, and toxic contaminants whose carbon–fluorine bonds resist conventional remediation. Two computational-experimental fields have grown rapidly and begun to converge on the problem: ...

item.title: RESEARCH ARTICLE Fast Isotopic [18F]Fluoride Exchange in Iminosulfur Oxydifluorides and Triflimidoyl Fluorides: A novel approach for [18F]tagging of primary amine-derived precursors
item.description: Direct incorporation of fluorine-18 into primary amine- derived molecules remains a major challenge in radiochemistry. Current strategies largely rely on multistep conjugation of electrophilic prosthetic groups, limiting automation and generating ...

item.title: A Novel Approach for Monitoring The Spatial Distribution and Quantitative Analysis of Micronutrients in Plant Tissues Using Laser Ablation Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry Imaging
item.description: Quantitative imaging of plant tissues by laser ablation inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (LA-ICP-MS) is hindered by the lack of matrix-matched calibration standards. Established approaches, such as gelatine or homogenized tissue blocks, do not ...

item.title: Spin Magnetoreception Through Redox-Active Residues and Nucleotides
item.description: The radical pair mechanism explains how low-intensity magnetic fields influence the coherent singlet–triplet interconversion of spin-correlated radical pairs formed by electron transfer in photoredox-active biological chromophores. Although this mechanism ...

item.title: MARS-RNA: An Automated Pipeline for Correlating RNA Molecular Dynamics Trajectories with Chemical Probing Data
item.description: Ribonucleic acid (RNA) molecules fold into complex, highly dynamic three-dimensional conformational structures that govern their biological activities. Chemical probing methods, such as SHAPE (selective 2’-hydroxyl acylation analyzed by primer extension), ...

item.title: Excited state dynamics of an air-stable low-spin carbene manganese(III) dual charge transfer chromophore
item.description: We report synthesis, structure and properties of a N-heterocyclic carbene pyridine manganese(III) complex featuring a low-spin ground state. The complex exhibits excellent stability under ambient conditions in the solid state and even dissolved in water. ...

item.title: Systematic Investigation of Reaction Parameters and Purification Strategies for DNA-Polymer Conjugates
item.description: DNA-polymer conjugates are an emerging class of biohybrid materials with applications in therapeutics and biosensing, yet quantitative guidelines for optimizing conjugation conversion and purification remain unreported. Here, we systematically investigate ...

item.title: Synthetic Pathway Modulation of Metals Ordering in Multivariate Metal-Organic Frameworks
Spin-Resolved Dielectric Bath Embedding Theory for Metallic Surfaces
item.description: We introduce spin-resolved dielectric bath embedding theory (SR-DBET), which incorporates a physics-informed embedding potential to describe attractive and repulsive interactions, a charge dielectric bath to capture charge polarization, and a spin ...

item.title: VPT2 Meets MLPs: IR and Raman Spectroscopy of Eight Molecules, Including 74-Atom Cholesterol, Using MACE-OFF and MACE-MDP
item.description: Vibrational second order perturbation theory is a widely used method to perform post-harmonic calculations of vibrational energies and infrared (IR) and Raman spectroscopy. We recently reported an application of this approach for vibrational energies ...

item.title: A Wearable Multimodal Platform for Monitoring Plant Heat Stress via Leaf VOCs and Relative Humidity
item.description: Heat stress limits plant productivity by disrupting transpiration, altering leaf microclimate, and activating metabolic pathways that increase volatile organic compound (VOC) emissions. VOC signatures, combined with leaf-level relative humidity (RH), ...

item.title: The effect of Pt concentration on lean methane oxidation over Pd-Pt/CeO2 catalysts depends on ceria faceting
item.description: Pd:Pt/CeO2catalysts supported on shape-controlled CeO2nanorods and nanocubes were investigated for lean methane oxidation to determine how Pt substitution and support morphology affect activity, stability, water inhibition, and redox behavior. Nanocube-...

item.title: Ruthenium-Catalyzed N-Dealkylation of Aromatic Amines
item.description: In industrial organic chemistry, amines and N-alkylamines represent crucial intermediates. Conversely, N-methyl amines are undesirable byproducts of recycling processes of polyamides or polyurethanes that involve methanolysis or hydrogenolysis steps. ...

item.title: CACHE Challenge #4: Targeting the TKB Domain of the E3 ligase CBLB, an Immuno-Oncology Target
item.description: The Critical Assessments of Computational Hit-finding Experiments (CACHE) are prospective benchmarking exercises where small molecule ligands predicted for a nominated protein of interest are procured and tested experimentally. In CACHE #4, 23 ...

item.title: Single-Molecule Fluorescence Reveals Catalytic Heterogeneity and Active Species in Copper Nanocatalyzed Click Reactions
item.description: The copper-catalyzed azide–alkyne cycloaddition (CuAAC) is a cornerstone of click chemistry and has been widely employed in many applications. However, its mechanism with heterogeneous catalysts remains incompletely understood. Catalytic reactions ...

item.title: Early Detection and Recovery of SCF Convergence Failures in Automated Quantum Chemistry Workflows via Time-Series Learning
item.description: Self-consistent field (SCF) convergence failures represent a major bottleneck in high-throughput workflows, particularly for open-shell systems and material discovery. To address this challenge, we introduce an autonomous pipeline that treats the ...

item.title: Programming Electrocatalytic Alkene Epoxidation via Potentiodynamic Control of Surface Oxygen
item.description: Electrocatalytic alkene epoxidation offers an attractive route to epoxides using water as an oxygen source at near ambient conditions, but direct electrochemical strategies often suffer from low selectivity and limited catalytic activity. Here, we ...

item.title: Infrared analysis suitable for high-throughput detection of keratin film formation from sheep wool material
item.description: Sheep wool represents an abundant and underutilized bioresource, primarily composed of keratin, with significant potential for the development of sustainable biomaterials. In this study, the formation of keratin films from wool was investigated using a ...

item.title: A Machine Learning QSAR Model for Predicting hTERT Telomerase Inhibitor Potency: ChEMBL Dataset Curation, Algorithm Benchmarking, and Prospective Application to Natural and Synthetic Anticancer Compounds
item.description: Human telomerase reverse transcriptase (hTERT) is reactivated in approximately 85–90% of human cancers, establishing it as one of the most broadly expressed oncological drug targets; yet no quantitative structure–activity relationship (QSAR) model trained ...

item.title: Systematic Benchmarking Dataset Generation for Graphical Model Reaction Templates
item.description: Computational kinetic modeling relies on accurate transition-state geometries and activation barriers. While machine learning models trained on ab initio data have shown promise for transition-state search and barrier prediction, they often struggle to ...

item.title: Spin Manifolds in the [Fe4S4(SCH3)4]2− Cluster from Localized Active Space State Interaction Singles and Pair-Density Functional Theory
item.description: Iron–sulfur clusters play central roles in biological photosynthesis, respiration and catalysis. The [Fe4S4(SCH3)4] 2− cluster possesses a dense manifold of low-lying electronic states arising from strong magnetic interactions among the Fe centers, making ...

item.title: Reprogrammed cobalt–hydride reactivity enables enantioselective alkene hydrosulfenation
item.description: Catalytic enantioselective functionalization of unactivated alkenes is a central strategy for building saturated, three-dimensional molecules, yet accessing aliphatic C(sp3)–S stereocenters remains difficult. Sulfenic acids (RSOH) offer a direct entry to ...

item.title: A Modular and Removable Radical Relay Platform for Remote Side-Chain C–H Editing of Amino Acids
item.description: Site-selective functionalization of inert side-chain C(sp3)–H bonds in amino acids and peptides remains a longstanding challenge owing to the high bond dissociation energies and similar reactivity profiles of multiple aliphatic C–H sites. Herein, we ...

item.title: Symmetry Breaking with a Proper Approximate Density Functional: Exact Ground-State Energy as a Close Lower Bound on the Computed Energy
item.description: In the widely-used Kohn-Sham density functional theory for the many-electron ground state, only the density functional for the exchange-correlation energy is approximated. While the exact density functional minimizes at the exact ground-state energy for ...

item.title: Dynamic vacancy redistribution shifts Pd nanoparticle coalescence from orientation-gated to defect-enabled on reduced CeO2(110)
item.description: Sintering deactivates supported catalysts, yet how mobile support defects couple to crystallographic transport anisotropy during nanoparticle coalescence remains poorly understood. Here, we develop a ternary Ce–O–Pd machine-learning interatomic potential (...

item.title: Grounding LLM Reasoning with Digital Modeling for Autonomous Chemical Discovery
item.description: The application of large language models (LLMs) to scientific research has raised growing expectations for autonomous discovery. Although LLMs are capable of scientific reasoning, literature investigation and hypothesis generation, they lack feedback ...

item.title: SabreSDN: A Complex-Valued 1D U-Net for Denoising SABRE-Hyperpolarized NMR Spectra at Low Concentration
item.description: Signal Amplification By Reversible Exchange (SABRE) can enhance nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) signals by several thousand-fold through para-hydrogen-derived hyperpolarization. However, at low substrate concentrations (50~$\mu$M and below), the signal-...

item.title: Electroosmotic Flow in Fractal Tree Networks with Spatially Varying Surface Charge
item.description: Electroosmotic transport through branching microchannel networks plays a central role in numerous charged biological porous media, membranes, and microscale physiological systems, yet existing optimization theories are largely restricted to uniformly ...

item.title: Catalytic C-F Bond Activation of Allylic Fluorides by Lewis or Brönsted Acid : A Real Synthesis Tool of Natural Products and Diversity Molecular Architectures
